<content>1: # Equation Paper (דף נוסחאות) - Feature Specification
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: Provide students with immediate access to relevant mathematical formulas during their interaction with the AI teacher. This mimics a real exam environment and reduces cognitive load by preventing students from needing to leave the chat to look up standard equations.
6: 
7: ## Requirements
8: 
9: ### FR-001: Formula Sheets Collection
10: 
11: Create a FormulaSheets collection in Payload CMS with:
12: - **name** (text, required): Human-readable name following pattern [Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version]
13: - **content** (richText, optional): Lexical rich text with LaTeX support
14: - **pdfFile** (upload, optional): Relationship to media collection for PDF files (max 5MB)
15: - **locale** (content locale): Localization field (he/en)
16: - **tenant** (tenant field): Multi-tenancy support
17: - **Access**: Admin-only create/read/update/delete
18: 
19: ### FR-002: Lesson Formula Sheet Field
20: 
21: Add optional `formulaSheet` field to Lessons collection:
22: - Type: relationship to FormulaSheets collection
23: - Purpose: Lesson-specific formula sheet override
24: 
25: ### FR-003: Course Default Formula Sheet Field
26: 
27: Add optional `defaultFormulaSheet` field to Courses collection:
28: - Type: relationship to FormulaSheets collection
29: - Purpose: Course-level default formula sheet
30: 
31: ### FR-004: Data Migration
32: 
33: Create one-time idempotent migration script to populate formula sheet content for course "כיתה יא - 4 יח'ל - 471" (course code 471) with content from Figma.
34: 
35: ### Visibility & Access
36: 
37: - Target Audience: The button and sheet are visible to all users (public or authenticated) who are viewing a published lesson
38: - Empty State: If neither the lesson nor the course has an attached formula sheet, the "דף נוסחאות" button must be hidden
39: - Localization: Formula sheets are localized per locale
40: - Fallback Rule: If a sheet is missing in the user's current locale (e.g., he), attempt to render the en version. If both are missing, hide the button
41: 
42: ### Content Precedence
43: 
44: The system follows a strict fallback hierarchy:
45: 
46: - Lesson Override: If a Lesson has a specific sheet attached, display it
47: - Course Default: If the Lesson has no sheet, use the parent Course default
48: - None: Hide the UI
49: 
50: ### Interaction Rules
51: 
52: - Persistence: Opening or closing the viewer must not interrupt the chat session, clear input text, or reset scroll positions
53: - Responsive Display: On mobile, the sheet opens as a full-screen drawer. On desktop, it renders as a side panel or modal (per Figma)
54: 
55: ### Content Formats
56: 
57: - Lexical richText: Must be rendered via the existing MathMarkdown component to support LaTeX formulas
58: - PDF: Max file size 5MB. Must be responsive and allow zoom/scroll on mobile viewports
59: 
60: ### Admin & Governance
61: 
62: - Shared Assets: Formula Sheets are shared entities. A single sheet (e.g., "Standard Trigonometry") can be linked to multiple courses or lessons
63: - Global Updates: Editing a shared sheet updates its content everywhere it is linked immediately
64: - Naming Convention: Admins must name sheets using the pattern: [Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version] (e.g., 471 - Calc - v1)
65: 
66: ### Edge Cases
67: 
68: - Missing PDF File: If a sheet is type PDF but the file is missing, display: "Formula sheet could not be loaded."
69: - Empty Content: If a RichText sheet is linked but has no content, hide the button
70: - Slow Load: Provide a loading skeleton while content is fetching
71: - Mobile PDF: On mobile, PDF viewer must support zoom and scroll interactions
72: - Locale Fallback: If lesson formula sheet exists only in en but user is on he locale, display en version
73: 
74: ## Acceptance Criteria
75: 
76: - [ ] Button styling matches Figma (Telescope theme) and is added to the chat
77: - [ ] "דף נוסחאות" button is hidden if no sheet is linked
78: - [ ] Lesson-level override correctly replaces Course-level default
79: - [ ] LaTeX equations render clearly in RichText mode using MathMarkdown
80: - [ ] Closing the viewer preserves the active chat input and scroll state
81: - [ ] PDF rendering supports zoom/scroll on mobile viewports
82: 
83: ## Non-Functional Requirements
84: 
85: ### NFR-001: Performance
86: - Formula sheet fetching must use efficient queries with depth=0 to avoid over-fetching
87: - Loading skeletons must match both PDF and RichText content types
88: 
89: ### NFR-002: Localization
90: - All user-facing strings must use translation keys
91: - Fallback pattern: he → en → hide button
92: 
93: ## Technical Constraints
94: 
95: - Frontend Scope: Only add the sheet toggle button within the chat interface and the presentation component for the content itself. No editing, searching, or management capabilities in the student-facing frontend
96: - Admin Management: Formula sheet CRUD operations are handled via Payload admin panel (not student-facing)
97: - TypeScript: Must run `pnpm generate:types` after modifying collections

(End of file - total 97 lines)
</content>

# Equation Paper (דף נוסחאות) - Feature Specification

## Overview

Provide students with immediate access to relevant mathematical formulas during their interaction with the AI teacher. This mimics a real exam environment and reduces cognitive load by preventing students from needing to leave the chat to look up standard equations.

## Requirements

### FR-001: Formula Sheets Collection

Create a FormulaSheets collection in Payload CMS with:
- **name** (text, required): Human-readable name following pattern [Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version]
- **content** (richText, optional): Lexical rich text with LaTeX support
- **pdfFile** (upload, optional): Relationship to media collection for PDF files (max 5MB)
- **locale** (content locale): Localization field (he/en)
- **tenant** (tenant field): Multi-tenancy support
- **Access**: Admin-only create/read/update/delete

### FR-002: Lesson Formula Sheet Field

<content>1: # Plan: Equation Paper (דף נוסחאות) Feature
2: 
3: ## Rerun Context
4: 
5: This is a rerun with minimal feedback ("Rerun requested via /cody rerun"). The previous plan was not found (first run was incomplete). This plan is written from scratch based on spec.md and clarified.md.
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Overview
10: 
11: This feature provides students access to mathematical formula sheets during chat sessions. It consists of:
12: 1. A new `FormulaSheets` Payload CMS collection (FR-001)
13: 2. A `formulaSheet` relationship field on `Lessons` (FR-002)
14: 3. A `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field on `Courses` (FR-003)
15: 4. A formula sheet viewer UI in the chat interface with precedence logic (lesson > course > hide)
16: 5. A migration script for course 471 content (FR-004)
17: 
18: ### Assumptions
19: - The formula sheet content format uses Payload's Lexical richText editor (same as `defaultLexical`)
20: - PDF files are uploaded to the existing `media` collection (no separate upload collection needed)
21: - The content locale field (`locale`) uses the existing `contentLocaleField` from `src/server/payload/fields/contentLocale.ts`
22: - The formula sheet viewer on mobile is a full-screen drawer; on desktop it's a side panel/modal
23: - The "דף נוסחאות" button is added as a toolbar item above the chat input in `ChatInterface`
24: - Rich text content rendering uses Payload's `RichText` component for Lexical output (not MathMarkdown, since Lexical is the format stored)
25: - For PDF rendering, the existing `PDFMedia` component at `src/ui/web/media/PDFMedia/index.tsx` is reused
26: - The migration script (Step 5) is deferred and can be a placeholder since actual Figma content is not available programmatically
27: 
28: ---
29: 
30: ## Step 1: Create FormulaSheets Collection (FR-001)
31: 
32: **Estimated time**: 15 minutes
33: 
34: **Files to Touch**:
35: - `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets.ts` (NEW)
36: - `src/payload.config.ts` (MODIFIED - lines 81-170, add import + register)
37: 
38: **Exact Behavior**:
39: - Create a new Payload collection with slug `formula-sheets`
40: - Fields:
41:   - `name` (text, required) — Human-readable name e.g. "471 - Calc - v1"
42:   - `content` (richText, optional) — Lexical rich text field for LaTeX formulas
43:   - `pdfFile` (upload, optional, relationTo: 'media') — PDF file reference
44:   - `locale` — use existing `contentLocaleField` (select: 'he'|'en', default 'he')
45:   - `tenant` — use existing `tenantField`
46: - Access: `adminOnly` for all CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
47: - Admin config: `useAsTitle: 'name'`, `defaultColumns: ['name', 'locale', 'tenant', 'updatedAt']`
48: - Enable timestamps
49: - Register in `payload.config.ts` collections array
50: 
51: **Tests** (integration test — FAIL before, PASS after):
52: 
53: - **Test file**: `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` (NEW)
54: - **Test 1**: `should create a formula sheet with name and content` — Creates a formula sheet via `payload.create({ collection: 'formula-sheets', data: { name: '471 - Calc - v1' }, overrideAccess: true })`. Asserts doc has `name`, `id`, `locale` defaults to 'he'.
55: - **Test 2**: `should enforce admin-only access on create` — Attempts `payload.create({ collection: 'formula-sheets', data: {...}, user: studentUser, overrideAccess: false })`. Expects rejection/forbidden.
56: - **Test 3**: `should allow pdfFile relationship to media` — Creates a formula sheet with `pdfFile` set to a media doc ID. Asserts the relationship is saved correctly.
57: 
58: **Acceptance Criteria**:
59: - [ ] FormulaSheets collection exists with slug `formula-sheets`
60: - [ ] All CRUD operations restricted to admin role
61: - [ ] `name` field is required, `content` and `pdfFile` are optional
62: - [ ] `locale` defaults to 'he' and accepts 'he'|'en'
63: - [ ] `tenant` auto-populates via existing tenant hook
64: - [ ] Collection registered in payload.config.ts
65: - [ ] `pnpm generate:types` succeeds with new collection
66: 
67: ---
68: 
69: ## Step 2: Add formulaSheet Field to Lessons and Courses (FR-002, FR-003)
70: 
71: **Estimated time**: 10 minutes
72: 
73: **Files to Touch**:
74: - `src/server/payload/collections/Lessons.ts` (MODIFIED — add field after line 238, before `slug`)
75: - `src/server/payload/collections/Courses.ts` (MODIFIED — add field after line 207, before `slug`)
76: 
77: **Exact Behavior**:
78: - **Lessons**: Add optional `formulaSheet` field:
79:   ```
80:   { name: 'formulaSheet', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'formula-sheets', admin: { position: 'sidebar', description: 'Lesson-specific formula sheet override' } }
81:   ```
82: - **Courses**: Add optional `defaultFormulaSheet` field:
83:   ```
84:   { name: 'defaultFormulaSheet', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'formula-sheets', admin: { position: 'sidebar', description: 'Default formula sheet for all lessons in this course' } }
85:   ```
86: 
87: **Tests** (integration test — extends `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):
88: 
89: - **Test 4**: `should link formula sheet to lesson` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a lesson with `formulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches lesson and asserts `formulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
90: - **Test 5**: `should link default formula sheet to course` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a course with `defaultFormulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches course and asserts `defaultFormulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
91: 
92: **Acceptance Criteria**:
93: - [ ] Lessons collection has optional `formulaSheet` relationship field
94: - [ ] Courses collection has optional `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field
95: - [ ] Both fields appear in admin sidebar
96: - [ ] Type generation succeeds (`pnpm generate:types`)
97: - [ ] Existing lessons/courses without the field continue to work (optional field)
98: 
99: ---
100: 
101: ## Step 3: Create Formula Sheet Data-Fetching Query and API Endpoint
102: 
103: **Estimated time**: 20 minutes
104: 
105: **Files to Touch**:
106: - `src/server/repos/queries/formula-sheets.ts` (NEW)
107: - `src/app/api/formula-sheet/route.ts` (NEW)
108: 
109: **Exact Behavior**:
110: 
111: ### Query function (`formula-sheets.ts`)
112: - `queryFormulaSheetForLesson({ lessonId, locale })`:
113:   1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0` → get `formulaSheet` ID
114:   2. If lesson has `formulaSheet`, fetch formula sheet by ID
115:   3. If not, get lesson's chapter → chapter's course → course's `defaultFormulaSheet`
116:   4. If formula sheet found, check its `locale` matches requested locale
117:   5. Locale fallback: if sheet not found for `locale`, try other locale ('he' → 'en' or 'en' → 'he')
118:   6. If no sheet found at all, return `null`
119:   7. Return formula sheet data: `{ id, name, content, pdfFile, locale }`
120: 
121: ### API route (`route.ts`)
122: - `GET /api/formula-sheet?lessonId=xxx`
123: - Response: `{ formulaSheet: { id, name, content, pdfFile, locale } | null }`
124: - Uses `queryFormulaSheetForLesson` internally
125: - No auth required (formula sheets are visible to all users viewing published lessons)
126: - Returns 400 if `lessonId` missing
127: - Returns 200 with `{ formulaSheet: null }` if no sheet found
128: 
129: **Tests** (integration test — `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):
130: 
131: - **Test 6**: `should resolve lesson-level formula sheet` — Creates course, chapter, lesson with formulaSheet set. Calls query function. Asserts returns the lesson's sheet (not course default).
132: - **Test 7**: `should fall back to course default when lesson has no sheet` — Creates course with defaultFormulaSheet, chapter, lesson (no formulaSheet). Calls query. Asserts returns course's default sheet.
133: - **Test 8**: `should return null when neither lesson nor course has a sheet` — Creates lesson/course without any sheets. Calls query. Asserts returns null.
134: - **Test 9**: `should handle locale fallback` — Creates formula sheet with locale 'en' only. Queries with locale 'he'. Asserts returns the 'en' sheet as fallback.
135: 
136: **Acceptance Criteria**:
137: - [ ] Query resolves lesson → course precedence correctly
138: - [ ] Locale fallback works: he → en → null
139: - [ ] API returns proper JSON response with formula sheet data
140: - [ ] API returns 400 for missing lessonId
141: - [ ] Uses `depth: 0` for efficient queries (FR: NFR-001)
142: 
143: ---
144: 
145: ## Step 4: Create Formula Sheet Viewer UI Components
146: 
147: **Estimated time**: 25 minutes
148: 
149: **Files to Touch**:
150: - `src/ui/web/chat/FormulaSheetButton/index.tsx` (NEW) — The toggle button
151: - `src/ui/web/chat/FormulaSheetViewer/index.tsx` (NEW) — The viewer panel/drawer
152: - `src/ui/web/chat/ChatInterface/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — lines 527-531, add toolbar with formula sheet button above input)
153: - `src/i18n/he.json` (MODIFIED — add translation keys under `courses`)
154: - `src/i18n/en.json` (MODIFIED — add translation keys under `courses`)
155: 
156: **Exact Behavior**:
157: 
158: ### FormulaSheetButton
159: - Client component (`'use client'`)
160: - Props: `{ lessonId: string; disabled?: boolean }`
161: - Fetches formula sheet data via `GET /api/formula-sheet?lessonId=xxx` using `useSWR` or `useEffect+fetch`
162: - Shows loading skeleton while fetching
163: - If `null` result → renders nothing (button hidden) — satisfies "hide if no sheet"
164: - If sheet found → renders button with text "דף נוסחאות" (from i18n key `courses.formulaSheet`)
165: - On click → opens FormulaSheetViewer
166: - Icon: use `FileText` or `BookOpen` from lucide-react
167: 
168: ### FormulaSheetViewer
169: - Client component (`'use client'`)
170: - Props: `{ sheet: FormulaSheetData; isOpen: boolean; onClose: () => void }`
171: - On mobile (`< 768px`): full-screen drawer from bottom
172: - On desktop: modal/side panel overlay
173: - Content rendering:
174:   - If `sheet.content` exists (richText): render via Payload's `RichText` component or serialized Lexical output. Since we're rendering Lexical rich text on the frontend, use the existing RichText serializer.
175:   - If `sheet.pdfFile` exists: render via existing `PDFMedia` component
176:   - If pdfFile is set but file is missing/broken: show "Formula sheet could not be loaded." error message
177: - Close button in header

180: ### ChatInterface Integration

182: - Insert a toolbar `div` between the "Chat Error Surface" section and the "Input Container" section (or above the input container)
183: - The toolbar contains `<FormulaSheetButton lessonId={lessonId} />` when `lessonId` is provided
184: - Opening/closing viewer must NOT affect chat state (input value, scroll position, messages)

186: ### i18n Keys

188: - `formulaSheet`: "דף נוסחאות" / "Formula Sheet"
189: - `formulaSheetClose`: "סגור" / "Close"
190: - `formulaSheetError`: "לא ניתן לטעון את דף הנוסחאות." / "Formula sheet could not be loaded."
191: - `formulaSheetLoading`: "טוען דף נוסחאות..." / "Loading formula sheet..."

193: **Tests** (unit tests — FAIL before, PASS after):

195: - **Test file**: `tests/unit/components/chat/FormulaSheetButton.test.tsx` (NEW)
196: - **Test 10**: `should render nothing when no formula sheet exists` — Mock fetch to return `{ formulaSheet: null }`. Render `<FormulaSheetButton lessonId="123" />`. Assert nothing is rendered in DOM.
197: - **Test 11**: `should render button when formula sheet exists` — Mock fetch to return valid sheet. Assert button with text "Formula Sheet" is visible.
198: - **Test 12**: `should not render when lessonId is not provided` — Render without lessonId. Assert nothing renders.
199: 
200: - **Test file**: `tests/unit/components/chat/FormulaSheetViewer.test.tsx` (NEW)
201: - **Test 13**: `should render rich text content when sheet has content` — Pass sheet with content. Assert content area is rendered.
202: - **Test 14**: `should render PDF viewer when sheet has pdfFile` — Pass sheet with pdfFile. Assert PDFMedia component or iframe is rendered.
203: - **Test 15**: `should show error when pdfFile is missing/invalid` — Pass sheet with pdfFile but broken URL. Assert error message displays.
204: 
205: **Acceptance Criteria**:
206: - [ ] "דף נוסחאות" button appears in chat toolbar when a formula sheet is available
207: - [ ] Button is hidden when no formula sheet is linked (neither lesson nor course)
208: - [ ] Viewer opens without affecting chat state (input, scroll, messages)
209: - [ ] Rich text content renders with LaTeX support
210: - [ ] PDF content renders using existing PDFMedia component
211: - [ ] Mobile: viewer opens as full-screen drawer
212: - [ ] Desktop: viewer opens as modal/panel
213: - [ ] Loading skeleton shown while fetching
214: - [ ] Error message shown for broken PDF
215: - [ ] All strings use i18n translation keys
216: 
217: ---
218: 
219: ## Step 5: Pass Formula Sheet Context From Lesson Page to ChatInterface
220: 
221: **Estimated time**: 15 minutes
222: 
223: **Files to Touch**:
224: - `src/app/(frontend)/courses/[courseSlug]/chapters/[chapterSlug]/lessons/[lessonSlug]/page.tsx` (MODIFIED — pass `lessonId` to ChatInterface, already done in most places)
225: - `src/app/(frontend)/courses/[courseSlug]/chapters/[chapterSlug]/lessons/[lessonSlug]/_components/ExercisesPager/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — ensure `lessonId` is passed through)
226: 
227: **Exact Behavior**:
228: - Verify all `<ChatInterface>` instances in lesson page pass `lessonId={lesson.id}` (most already do)
229: - The `FormulaSheetButton` inside `ChatInterface` uses `lessonId` to fetch the appropriate formula sheet via the API
230: - No server-side pre-fetching needed — the button does client-side fetching
231: 
232: **Tests** (integration test — `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):
233: 
234: - **Test 16**: `should resolve formula sheet through full lesson→chapter→course hierarchy` — Creates full hierarchy: course with `defaultFormulaSheet`, chapter, lesson. Uses query function to verify correct sheet is resolved through the chain.
235: 
236: **Acceptance Criteria**:

241: ---
242: 
243: ## Step 6: Data Migration Script for Course 471 (FR-004)
244:

262: **Tests** (integration test — `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):

265: 
266: **Acceptance Criteria**:
267: - [ ] Migration creates formula sheet for course 471
268: - [ ] Migration is idempotent (running twice doesn't create duplicates)
269: - [ ] Formula sheet linked to course 471 as `defaultFormulaSheet`
270: - [ ] Migration can be safely called from `onInit`
271: 
272: ---
273: 
274: ## Step 7: Run Type Generation and Quality Checks
275: 
276: **Estimated time**: 5 minutes
277: 
278: **Files to Touch**:
279: - `src/payload-types.ts` (AUTO-GENERATED)
280: 
281: **Exact Behavior**:
282: - Run `pnpm generate:types` to regenerate types with new FormulaSheets collection
283: - Run `pnpm generate:importmap` to update admin import map
284: - Run `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` to verify TypeScript correctness

287: **Tests**: No new tests. This step validates all previous work compiles.

289: **Acceptance Criteria**:

291: - [ ] TypeScript compilation passes with no errors
292: - [ ] Lint passes
293: - [ ] All integration tests pass: `pnpm test:int -- --grep "formula-sheet"`
294: 
295: ---
296: 
297: ## Test Summary
298: 
299: | # | Test Name | Type | File |
300: |---|-----------|------|------|
301: | 1 | Create formula sheet with name and content | Integration | `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` |
302: | 2 | Enforce admin-only access on create | Integration | `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` |
303: | 3 | Allow pdfFile relationship to media | Integration | `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` |
304: | 4 | Link formula sheet to lesson | Integration | `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` |

# Plan: Equation Paper (דף נוסחאות) Feature

## Rerun Context

This is a rerun with minimal feedback ("Rerun requested via /cody rerun"). The previous plan was not found (first run was incomplete). This plan is written from scratch based on spec.md and clarified.md.

---

## Overview

This feature provides students access to mathematical formula sheets during chat sessions. It consists of:
1. A new `FormulaSheets` Payload CMS collection (FR-001)
2. A `formulaSheet` relationship field on `Lessons` (FR-002)
3. A `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field on `Courses` (FR-003)
4. A formula sheet viewer UI in the chat interface with precedence logic (lesson > course > hide)
5. A migration script for course 471 content (FR-004)

### Assumptions
- The formula sheet content format uses Payload's Lexical richText editor (same as `defaultLexical`)
- PDF files are uploaded to the existing `media` collection (no separate upload collection needed)

<content>1: import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'

12:   access: {
13:     create: adminOnly,
14:     delete: adminOnly,

17:   },
18:   hooks: {
19:     beforeChange: [
20:       ({ data }) => {
21:         if (data?.slug) {
22:           data.slug = data.slug.trim()
23:         }
24:         if (data?.title && !data?.slug) {
25:           // Generate unique slug from title
26:           // Include timestamp for uniqueness, falling back to random if timestamp not available
27:           const timestamp =
28:             typeof data.createdAt === 'string'
29:               ? data.createdAt.replace(/[^0-9]/g, '').slice(-6)
30:               : Date.now().toString().slice(-6)
31:           data.slug = `${formatSlug(data.title)}-${timestamp}`

33:         return data

<content>1: import type { Payload } from 'payload'
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Backfills the adminTitle field for chapters that don't have it set.
5:  *
6:  * This migration ensures all existing chapters have a computed adminTitle
7:  * in the format: "Chapter Title — Course Title"
8:  *
9:  * This is needed because the afterRead hook fails when Payload strips fields
10:  * via select: { adminTitle: true } (used by admin relationship dropdowns).
11:  *
12:  * Once this migration runs, all chapters will have adminTitle persisted in DB,
13:  * so relationship dropdowns will display the correct label without N+1 queries.
14:  *
15:  * @param payload - The Payload instance
16:  * @returns Object with counts of updated, skipped, and errored chapters
17:  */
18: export async function backfillChapterAdminTitles(
19:   payload: Payload,
20: ): Promise<{ updated: number; skipped: number; errors: number }> {
21:   let updated = 0
22:   let skipped = 0
23:   let errors = 0
24:   const batchSize = 500
25:   let page = 1
26:   let hasMore = true
27: 
28:   while (hasMore) {
29:     try {
30:       // Query chapters that need adminTitle backfilled
31:       const result = await payload.find({
32:         collection: 'chapters',
33:         where: {
34:           or: [
35:             { adminTitle: { equals: null } },
36:             { adminTitle: { equals: '' } },
37:             { adminTitle: { exists: false } },
38:           ],
39:         },
40:         limit: batchSize,
41:         page,
42:         depth: 0,
43:         overrideAccess: true,
44:       })
45: 
46:       const chapters = result.docs
47: 
48:       if (chapters.length === 0) {
49:         hasMore = false
50:         break
51:       }
52: 
53:       // Process each chapter in batch
54:       for (const chapter of chapters) {
55:         try {
56:           let courseTitle: string | null = null
57: 
58:           // Fetch the related course to get its title
59:           if (chapter.course && typeof chapter.course === 'string') {
60:             try {
61:               const course = await payload.findByID({
62:                 collection: 'courses',
63:                 id: chapter.course,
64:                 depth: 0,
65:                 overrideAccess: true,
66:               })
67:               courseTitle = (course as { title?: string })?.title || null
68:             } catch {
69:               // If course lookup fails, we'll use just the chapter title
70:               payload.logger?.warn(
71:                 `Could not fetch course ${chapter.course} for chapter ${chapter.id}`,
72:               )
73:             }
74:           }
75: 
76:           const chapterTitle = chapter.title || 'Untitled'
77:           const adminTitle = courseTitle ? `${chapterTitle} — ${courseTitle}` : chapterTitle
78: 
79:           // Update the chapter with adminTitle set
80:           // Use skipAdminTitleRecompute context to avoid triggering the beforeChange hook's redundant lookup
81:           await payload.update({
82:             collection: 'chapters',
83:             id: chapter.id,
84:             data: { adminTitle },
85:             context: { skipAdminTitleRecompute: true },
86:           })
87: 
88:           updated++
89:         } catch {
90:           errors++
91:           payload.logger?.warn(`Could not backfill adminTitle for chapter ${chapter.id}`)
92:         }
93:       }
94: 
95:       // Check if we need more pages
96:       if (chapters.length < batchSize) {
97:         hasMore = false
98:       } else {
99:         page++
100:       }
101:     } catch {
102:       payload.logger?.error('Error querying chapters for adminTitle backfill')
103:       errors++
104:       hasMore = false
105:     }
106:   }
107: 
108:   // Count already-completed chapters for logging
109:   try {
110:     const { totalDocs } = await payload.find({
111:       collection: 'chapters',
112:       where: {
113:         and: [
114:           { adminTitle: { exists: true } },
115:           { adminTitle: { not_equals: null } },
116:           { adminTitle: { not_equals: '' } },
117:         ],
118:       },
119:       limit: 0,
120:       overrideAccess: true,
121:     })
122:     skipped = totalDocs
123:   } catch {
124:     // Ignore counting errors - we still updated what we could
125:   }
126: 
127:   return { updated, skipped, errors }
128: }
129: 
130: /**
131:  * onInit wrapper for the backfill migration.
132:  *
133:  * This ensures the backfill runs automatically on server startup.
134:  * It's idempotent - it only updates chapters that need it.
135:  */
136: export async function runBackfillOnInit(payload: Payload): Promise<void> {
137:   const { updated, skipped, errors } = await backfillChapterAdminTitles(payload)
138: 
139:   if (updated > 0 || errors > 0) {
140:     payload.logger?.info(
141:       `Backfilled ${updated} chapter adminTitles (${skipped} already set, ${errors} errors)`,
142:     )
143:   }
144: }

(End of file - total 144 lines)

/**
 * Backfills the adminTitle field for chapters that don't have it set.

 * Once this migration runs, all chapters will have adminTitle persisted in DB,
 * so relationship dropdowns will display the correct label without N+1 queries.
 *
 * @param payload - The Payload instance
 * @returns Object with counts of updated, skipped, and errored chapters

126:     url: databaseUrl,
127:     connectOptions: {
128:       // Hardened connection pool configuration to prevent Atlas connection exhaustion
129:       // Tests: maxPoolSize=5 (default)
130:       // Production: maxPoolSize=2 (default, configurable via MONGODB_MAX_POOL_SIZE)
131:       // Rationale: With 500 connection limit, this allows 250 concurrent serverless instances
132:       // vs only 5 instances with the previous maxPoolSize=100 setting
133:       maxPoolSize: parseInt(
134:         process.env.MONGODB_MAX_POOL_SIZE ?? (process.env.VITEST ? '5' : '2'),
135:         10,
136:       ),
137:       // Allow pool to fully drain when idle
138:       minPoolSize: 0,
139:       // Close idle connections after 10 seconds
140:       maxIdleTimeMS: 10000,
141:     },
142:   }),
143:   collections: [
144:     Pages,
145:     Categories,
146:     ConfigSecrets,
147:     ConfigValues,
148:     ConfigAuditLogs,
149:     Conversations,
150:     GuestSessions,

171:   cors: [getServerSideURL()].filter(Boolean),
172:   globals: [Header, Footer],
173:   plugins,
174:   secret: process.env.PAYLOAD_SECRET,
175:   sharp,
176:   typescript: {
177:     outputFile: path.resolve(dirname, 'payload-types.ts'),
178:   },
179:   endpoints: [
180:     {
181:       path: '/exercises/import',
182:       method: 'post',
183:       handler: (req: PayloadRequest) => {
184:         // Route based on whether lessonId query param exists
185:         const url = new URL(req.url || 'http://localhost')
186:         if (url.searchParams.has('lessonId')) {
187:           return importExerciseFromLesson(req)
188:         }
189:         return importExerciseFromImage(req)
190:       },
191:     },
192:   ],
193:   jobs: {
194:     access: {
195:       run: ({ req }: { req: PayloadRequest }): boolean => {
196:         // Allow logged in users to execute this endpoint (default)
197:         if (req.user) return true
198: 
199:         // If there is no logged in user, then check
200:         // for the Vercel Cron secret to be present as an
201:         // Authorization header:
202:         const authHeader = req.headers.get('authorization')
203:         return authHeader === `Bearer ${process.env.CRON_SECRET}`
204:       },
205:     },
206:     tasks: [pdfToExercisesTask, pdfToExercisesV2Task],
207:     // Expose jobs collection in admin panel for monitoring conversion jobs
208:     jobsCollectionOverrides: ({ defaultJobsCollection }) => ({
209:       ...defaultJobsCollection,
210:       admin: {
211:         ...defaultJobsCollection.admin,
212:         hidden: false, // Make visible in admin sidebar
213:         group: 'System', // Group with other system collections
214:         defaultColumns: [
215:           'taskSlug',
216:           'inputCtx',
217:           'status',
218:           'progress',
219:           'hasErrors',
220:           'createdAt',
221:           'completedAt',
222:         ],
223:       },
224:       access: {
225:         ...defaultJobsCollection.access,
226:         // Admin-only access
227:         read: ({ req }) => isAdminUser(req),
228:         update: ({ req }) => isAdminUser(req),
229:         delete: ({ req }) => isAdminUser(req),
230:       },
231:       hooks: {
232:         afterRead: [
233:           // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any -- args.doc type comes from Payload internal types
234:           (args: any) => {
235:             const job = args.doc as unknown as JobDocument
236:             // Compute display fields for admin UI
237: 
238:             // status: Compute from MongoDB fields
239:             let status = 'queued'
240:             if (job?.processing) {
241:               status = 'running'
242:             } else if (job?.hasError) {
243:               status = 'failed'
244:             } else if (job?.completedAt) {
245:               status = 'completed'
246:             }
247: 
248:             // inputCtx: Show lessonId prefix for quick identification
249:             const lessonId = job?.input?.ctx?.lessonId
250:             const inputCtx = lessonId ? `Lesson: ${lessonId.slice(0, 8)}...` : '—'
251: 
252:             // progress: Show segments progress if available
253:             let progress = '—'
254:             const output = job?.output as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
255:             if (output?.segmentsTotal && typeof output.segmentsTotal === 'number') {
256:               progress = `${(output.segmentsDone as number) || 0}/${output.segmentsTotal} segments`
257:             }
258: 
259:             // hasErrors: Show ✅ or ❌
260:             const errors = output?.errors as unknown[] | undefined
261:             const hasErrors = (errors?.length ?? 0) > 0 ? '❌' : '✅'
262: 
263:             return {
264:               ...job,
265:               status,
266:               inputCtx,
267:               progress,
268:               hasErrors,
269:             }
270:           },
271:         ],
272:       },
273:     }),
274:   },
275:   onInit: async (payload) => {
276:     // Ensure default tenant exists BEFORE seedTeacherProfiles runs
277:     // This is required because TeacherProfilesSeed needs a tenant to link prompts to
278:     const defaultTenantSlug = process.env.DEFAULT_TENANT_SLUG || 'default'
279:     const existingTenant = await payload.find({
280:       collection: 'tenants',
281:       where: { slug: { equals: defaultTenantSlug } },
282:       limit: 1,
283:       overrideAccess: true,
284:     })
285: 
286:     if (existingTenant.totalDocs === 0) {
287:       await payload.create({
288:         collection: 'tenants',
289:         data: {
290:           name: 'Default',
291:           slug: defaultTenantSlug,
292:           status: 'active',
293:         },
294:         overrideAccess: true,
295:       })
296:       payload.logger.info(`[onInit] Created default tenant "${defaultTenantSlug}"`)
297:     }
298: 
299:     await runBackfillOnInit(payload)
300:     await seedTeacherProfiles(payload)
301:   },
302: })

2: import { queryCourseBySlug } from '@/server/repos/queries/courses'
3: import { queryLessonBySlug } from '@/server/repos/queries/lessons'
4: import {

7:   queryExercisesByLesson,
8: } from '@/server/repos/queries/exercises'
9: import { queryMediaByIds } from '@/server/repos/queries/media'
10: import { extractAllMediaIds } from '@/ui/web/exerciserenderer/utils/extractMediaIds'
11: import { ExercisesPager } from '../../_components/ExercisesPager'
12: 
13: const OBJECT_ID_REGEX = /^[0-9a-fA-F]{24}$/
14: 
15: function isObjectId(value: string): boolean {
16:   return OBJECT_ID_REGEX.test(value)
17: }
18: 
19: interface ExercisePageProps {
20:   params: Promise<{
21:     courseSlug: string
22:     chapterSlug: string
23:     lessonSlug: string
24:     exerciseSlug: string
25:   }>
26: }
27: 
28: async function resolveExercise(lessonId: string, param: string) {
29:   const exerciseBySlug = await queryExerciseBySlug({ lessonId, slug: param })
30: 
31:   if (exerciseBySlug) {
32:     return { exercise: exerciseBySlug, mode: 'slug' as const }
33:   }
34: 
35:   if (isObjectId(param)) {
36:     const exerciseById = await queryExerciseById({ id: param })
37: 
38:     if (exerciseById) {
39:       const exerciseLesson = typeof exerciseById.lesson === 'string' ? null : exerciseById.lesson
40:       if (exerciseLesson && exerciseLesson.id === lessonId) {
41:         if (exerciseById.slug) {
42:           return {
43:             exercise: exerciseById,
44:             mode: 'redirect' as const,
45:             canonicalSlug: exerciseById.slug,
46:           }
47:         }
48:         return { exercise: exerciseById, mode: 'id' as const }
49:       }
50:     }
51:   }
52: 
53:   return { exercise: null, mode: 'not-found' as const }
54: }
55: 
56: export default async function ExercisePage({ params }: ExercisePageProps) {
57:   const { courseSlug, chapterSlug, lessonSlug, exerciseSlug } = await params
58: 
59:   const [course, lesson] = await Promise.all([
60:     queryCourseBySlug({ slug: courseSlug }),
61:     queryLessonBySlug({ slug: lessonSlug }),
62:   ])
63: 
64:   if (!course || !lesson) {
65:     notFound()
66:   }
67: 
68:   const lessonChapter = typeof lesson.chapter === 'string' ? null : lesson.chapter
69:   const lessonCourseId = lessonChapter
70:     ? typeof lessonChapter.course === 'string'
71:       ? lessonChapter.course
72:       : lessonChapter.course?.id
73:     : null
74: 
75:   if (!lessonCourseId || lessonCourseId !== course.id) {
76:     notFound()
77:   }
78: 
79:   if (!lessonChapter || lessonChapter.slug !== chapterSlug) {
80:     notFound()
81:   }
82: 
83:   const [resolved, exercises] = await Promise.all([
84:     resolveExercise(lesson.id, exerciseSlug),
85:     queryExercisesByLesson({ lessonId: lesson.id }),
86:   ])
87: 
88:   if (resolved.mode === 'not-found' || !resolved.exercise) {
89:     notFound()
90:   }
91: 
92:   if (resolved.mode === 'redirect') {
93:     const redirectUrl = `/courses/${courseSlug}/chapters/${chapterSlug}/lessons/${lessonSlug}/exercises/${resolved.canonicalSlug}`
94:     redirect(redirectUrl)
95:   }
96: 
97:   const backUrl = `/courses/${courseSlug}/chapters/${chapterSlug}/lessons/${lessonSlug}`
98: 
99:   const mediaMap = await queryMediaByIds(extractAllMediaIds(exercises))
100: 
101:   return (
102:     <ExercisesPager
103:       exercises={exercises}
104:       lessonTitle={lesson.title}
105:       backUrl={backUrl}
106:       courseSlug={courseSlug}
107:       chapterSlug={chapterSlug}
108:       lessonSlug={lessonSlug}
109:       lessonId={lesson.id}
110:       mediaMap={mediaMap}
111:     />
112:   )
113: }
114: 
115: export async function generateMetadata({ params }: ExercisePageProps) {
116:   const { courseSlug, chapterSlug, lessonSlug, exerciseSlug } = await params
117: 
118:   const [course, lesson] = await Promise.all([
119:     queryCourseBySlug({ slug: courseSlug }),
120:     queryLessonBySlug({ slug: lessonSlug }),
121:   ])
122: 
123:   if (!course || !lesson) {
124:     return {
125:       title: 'Exercise Not Found',
126:     }
127:   }
128: 
129:   const lessonChapter = typeof lesson.chapter === 'string' ? null : lesson.chapter
130:   const lessonCourseId = lessonChapter
131:     ? typeof lessonChapter.course === 'string'
132:       ? lessonChapter.course
133:       : lessonChapter.course?.id
134:     : null
135: 
136:   if (!lessonCourseId || lessonCourseId !== course.id) {
137:     return {
138:       title: 'Exercise Not Found',
139:     }
140:   }
141: 
142:   if (!lessonChapter || lessonChapter.slug !== chapterSlug) {
143:     return {
144:       title: 'Exercise Not Found',
145:     }
146:   }
147: 
148:   const resolved = await resolveExercise(lesson.id, exerciseSlug)
149: 
150:   if (resolved.mode === 'not-found' || !resolved.exercise) {
151:     return {
152:       title: 'Exercise Not Found',
153:     }
154:   }
155: 
156:   if (resolved.mode === 'redirect') {
157:     return {
158:       title: 'Exercise Not Found',
159:     }
160:   }

<content>1: import type { CollectionBeforeValidateHook } from 'payload'
2: 
3: import { inferMediaType, validateMimeType } from '@/infra/media/inferMediaType'
4: import { MediaType, SIZE_LIMITS } from '@/infra/media/types'
5: 
6: export const validateMediaUploadHook: CollectionBeforeValidateHook = async ({
7:   data,
8:   operation,
9:   req,
10: }) => {
11:   if (operation !== 'create') return data
12: 
13:   const mimeType = data?.mimeType
14:   const filename = data?.filename
15:   const filesize = data?.filesize
16:   const type = data?.type || inferMediaType(mimeType, filename)
17: 
18:   // Guard: skip validation if file exists but data lacks metadata (client upload edge case)
19:   if (req.file && (!mimeType || !filename)) {
20:     return data
21:   }
22: 
23:   // External type should not have file upload
24:   if (type === MediaType.External) {
25:     if (!data?.externalUrl) {
26:       throw new Error('External media requires an external URL')
27:     }
28:     // Set filename from URL hostname if not provided
29:     if (!data.filename) {
30:       try {
31:         data.filename = new URL(data.externalUrl).hostname
32:       } catch {
33:         data.filename = 'External'
34:       }
35:     }
36:     return data
37:   }
38: 
39:   // Non-external types require a file
40:   if (!mimeType && !filename) {
41:     throw new Error(
42:       'A file is required for non-external media types. Please select a file to upload.',
43:     )
44:   }
45: 
46:   // Validate MIME type against allowlist
47:   if (mimeType && type !== MediaType.Other) {
48:     if (!validateMimeType(mimeType, type)) {
49:       data.type = MediaType.Other
50:     }
51:   }
52: 
53:   // Enforce size limits
54:   if (filesize && type && type in SIZE_LIMITS) {
55:     const maxSize = SIZE_LIMITS[type as MediaType]
56:     if (maxSize && filesize > maxSize) {
57:       const sizeMB = Math.round(filesize / 1024 / 1024)
58:       const maxSizeMB = Math.round(maxSize / 1024 / 1024)
59:       throw new Error(`File size (${sizeMB}MB) exceeds maximum for ${type} (${maxSizeMB}MB)`)
60:     }
61:   }
62: 
63:   return data
64: }

(End of file - total 64 lines)
</content>

<content>1: // @vitest-environment jsdom
2: import { ChatMessage } from '@/ui/web/chat/hooks/useNotebookChat'
3: import { ChatRole } from '@/infra/llm/chat-message-role'
4: import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
5: import * as fs from 'node:fs'
6: import * as path from 'node:path'
7: 
8: describe('ChatMessage ID validation', () => {
9:   describe('Test 1: Verify ChatMessage has a non-empty id string property', () => {
10:     it('should have a non-empty id property that is a string', () => {
11:       const message: ChatMessage = {
12:         id: 'test-id-123',
13:         role: ChatRole.User,
14:         content: 'Hello',
15:       }
16: 
17:       expect(typeof message.id).toBe('string')
18:       expect(message.id.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
19:     })
20: 
21:     it('should validate ChatMessage type has id as required string field', () => {
22:       // Test that the ChatMessage interface enforces non-empty id
23:       const validMessage: ChatMessage = {
24:         id: crypto.randomUUID(),
25:         role: ChatRole.Assistant,
26:         content: 'Test response',
27:       }
28: 
29:       expect(validMessage.id).toBeDefined()
30:       expect(typeof validMessage.id).toBe('string')
31:       expect(validMessage.id).not.toBe('')
32:     })
33:   })
34: 
35:   describe('Test 2: All message IDs in the array are unique (no duplicates)', () => {
36:     it('should generate unique IDs for multiple messages', () => {
37:       const messages: ChatMessage[] = [
38:         { id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: ChatRole.User, content: 'Message 1' },
39:         { id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: ChatRole.Assistant, content: 'Message 2' },
40:         { id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: ChatRole.User, content: 'Message 3' },
41:       ]
42: 
43:       const ids = messages.map((m) => m.id)
44:       const uniqueIds = new Set(ids)
45: 
46:       expect(uniqueIds.size).toBe(ids.length)
47:     })
48:   })
49: })
50: 
51: describe('ChatInterface static analysis - React keys', () => {
52:   const chatInterfacePath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), 'src/ui/web/chat/ChatInterface/index.tsx')
53: 
54:   describe('Test 3: ChatInterface does not use key={idx} or key={index} for messages', () => {
55:     it('should NOT use key={idx} or key={index} in messages.map', () => {
56:       const sourceCode = fs.readFileSync(chatInterfacePath, 'utf-8')
57: 
58:       // Check for problematic patterns like key={idx} or key={index} in message rendering
59:       const badPatterns = [
60:         /messages\.map\s*\(\s*\([^)]*,\s*idx\s*\)\s*=>[^}]*key=\{idx\}/,
61:         /messages\.map\s*\(\s*\([^)]*,\s*index\s*\)\s*=>[^}]*key=\{index\}/,
62:         /messages\.map\s*\(\s*\(\w+,\s*idx\)/,
63:         /messages\.map\s*\(\s*\(\w+,\s*index\)/,
64:       ]
65: 
66:       for (const pattern of badPatterns) {
67:         expect(sourceCode).not.toMatch(pattern)
68:       }
69:     })
70:   })
71: 
72:   describe('Test 4: ChatInterface uses key={msg.id} for messages', () => {
73:     it('should use key={msg.id} or key={messageId} for messages', () => {
74:       const sourceCode = fs.readFileSync(chatInterfacePath, 'utf-8')
75: 
76:       // Check for correct pattern: key={msg.id} or key={messageId}
77:       const goodPatterns = [
78:         /messages\.map\s*\(\s*\(\s*\w+\s*\)\s*=>[^}]*key=\{\w+\.id\}/,
79:         /key=\{\s*\w+\.id\s*\}/,
80:       ]
81: 
82:       // Should have at least one correct pattern
83:       const hasCorrectPattern = goodPatterns.some((pattern) => pattern.test(sourceCode))
84:       expect(hasCorrectPattern).toBe(true)
85:     })
86:   })
87: 
88:   describe('Test 5: ChatInterface does not use key={mediaIdx} or key={assetIdx}', () => {
89:     it('should NOT use key={mediaIdx} or key={assetIdx} in media/asset rendering', () => {
90:       const sourceCode = fs.readFileSync(chatInterfacePath, 'utf-8')
91: 
92:       // Check for bad patterns in media and chatAssets rendering
93:       const badPatterns = [
94:         /msg\.media\.map\s*\(\s*\([^)]*,\s*mediaIdx\s*\)/,
95:         /msg\.chatAssets\.map\s*\(\s*\([^)]*,\s*assetIdx\s*\)/,
96:         /key=\{mediaIdx\}/,
97:         /key=\{assetIdx\}/,
98:       ]
99: 
100:       for (const pattern of badPatterns) {
101:         expect(sourceCode).not.toMatch(pattern)
102:       }
103: 
104:       // Should use proper keys like key={mediaItem.mediaId} or key={asset.chatAssetId}
105:       const goodMediaKeyPattern = /key=\{\s*\w+\.mediaId\s*\}/
106:       const goodAssetKeyPattern = /key=\{\s*\w+\.chatAssetId\s*\}/
107: 
108:       expect(sourceCode).toMatch(goodMediaKeyPattern)
109:       expect(sourceCode).toMatch(goodAssetKeyPattern)
110:     })
111:   })
112: })
113: 
114: describe('CollectionArchive static analysis - React keys', () => {
115:   const collectionArchivePath = path.resolve(
116:     process.cwd(),
117:     'src/ui/web/CollectionArchive/index.tsx',
118:   )
119: 
120:   describe('Test 6: CollectionArchive uses key={result.slug} instead of key={index}', () => {
121:     it('should use key={result.slug} for posts mapping', () => {
122:       const sourceCode = fs.readFileSync(collectionArchivePath, 'utf-8')
123: 
124:       // Check for correct pattern: key={result.slug}
125:       const goodPattern = /key=\{\s*result\.slug\s*\}/
126:       expect(sourceCode).toMatch(goodPattern)
127:     })
128: 
129:     it('should NOT use key={index} or key={i} for posts mapping', () => {
130:       const sourceCode = fs.readFileSync(collectionArchivePath, 'utf-8')
131: 
132:       // Check for bad patterns
133:       const badPatterns = [
134:         /key=\{index\}/,
135:         /key=\{i\}/,
136:         /posts\.map\s*\(\s*\(\s*\w+,\s*index\s*\)/,
137:         /posts\.map\s*\(\s*\(\s*\w+,\s*i\s*\)/,
138:       ]
139: 
140:       for (const pattern of badPatterns) {
141:         expect(sourceCode).not.toMatch(pattern)
142:       }
143:     })
144:   })
145: })

(End of file - total 145 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import '@/infra/config/server-init'
2: 
3: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
4: import { queryChaptersByGrade } from '@/server/repos/queries/chapters'
5: import { getPayload } from 'payload'
6: import configPromise from '@payload-config'
7: import type { Lesson } from '@/payload-types'
8: import { DEFAULT_PAGE_ACCESS_TYPE } from '@/server/constants/access-types'
9: import { SystemParams } from '@/infra/config/system-params'
10: 
11: export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
12:   const searchParams = request.nextUrl.searchParams
13:   const grade = searchParams.get('grade')
14: 
15:   if (!grade) {
16:     return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Grade parameter is required' }, { status: 400 })
17:   }
18: 
19:   try {
20:     const chapters = await queryChaptersByGrade({ gradeLevel: grade })
21:     const course = chapters[0]?.course
22:     const courseObj = typeof course === 'object' && course !== null ? course : null
23:     const courseSlug = courseObj && 'slug' in courseObj ? (courseObj.slug as string) : ''
24:     const courseId = courseObj && 'id' in courseObj ? (courseObj.id as string) : ''
25:     const courseTitle = courseObj && 'title' in courseObj ? (courseObj.title as string) : ''
26:     const courseLabel =
27:       courseObj && 'courseLabel' in courseObj ? (courseObj.courseLabel as string) : ''
28:     const coursePageAccessType =
29:       courseObj && 'pageAccessType' in courseObj
30:         ? (courseObj.pageAccessType ?? DEFAULT_PAGE_ACCESS_TYPE)
31:         : DEFAULT_PAGE_ACCESS_TYPE
32: 
33:     // Fetch all lessons for all chapters (batch query for efficiency)
34:     const chapterIds = chapters.map((chapter) => chapter.id)
35:     let lessons: Lesson[] = []
36: 
37:     if (chapterIds.length > 0) {
38:       const payload = await getPayload({ config: configPromise })
39:       const lessonsResult = await payload.find({
40:         collection: 'lessons',
41:         where: {
42:           and: [
43:             {
44:               chapter: {
45:                 in: chapterIds,
46:               },
47:             },
48:             {
49:               status: {
50:                 equals: 'published',
51:               },
52:             },
53:             {
54:               isActive: {
55:                 equals: true,
56:               },
57:             },
58:           ],
59:         },
60:         sort: 'order',
61:         limit: 1000,
62:         pagination: false,
63:         depth: 2,
64:       })
65:       lessons = lessonsResult.docs
66:     }
67: 
68:     // Group lessons by chapter
69:     const lessonsByChapter: Record<string, Lesson[]> = {}
70:     lessons.forEach((lesson) => {
71:       const chapterId = typeof lesson.chapter === 'string' ? lesson.chapter : lesson.chapter?.id
72:       if (chapterId) {
73:         if (!lessonsByChapter[chapterId]) {
74:           lessonsByChapter[chapterId] = []
75:         }
76:         lessonsByChapter[chapterId].push(lesson)
77:       }
78:     })
79: 
80:     // Attach lessons to chapters
81:     const chaptersWithLessons = chapters.map((chapter) => ({
82:       ...chapter,
83:       lessons: lessonsByChapter[chapter.id] || [],
84:     }))
85: 
86:     const [gatedDelayMs, gatedWarningMs] = await Promise.all([
87:       SystemParams.getGatedDelayMs(),
88:       SystemParams.getGatedWarningMs(),
89:     ])
90: 
91:     return NextResponse.json({
92:       chapters: chaptersWithLessons,
93:       courseSlug,
94:       courseId,
95:       courseTitle,
96:       courseLabel,
97:       coursePageAccessType,
98:       gatedDelayMs,
99:       gatedWarningMs,
100:     })
101:   } catch (error) {
102:     const { captureAndRespond } = await import('@/server/api/capture-and-respond')
103:     return captureAndRespond(error, { route: '/api/chapters/by-grade' })
104:   }
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import type { Access } from 'payload'
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Access control helper for collections with custom `status` field.
5:  *
6:  * - If req.user is present (authenticated), allows full read access.
7:  * - If req.user is absent (anonymous), restricts to:
8:  *   - status = 'published'
9:  *   - isActive = true
10:  *
11:  * IMPORTANT: This helper uses the custom `status` field (not Payload's `_status`).
12:  * Use this for courses, chapters, lessons collections that have custom status.
13:  */
14: export const publishedAndActive: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => {
15:   // Authenticated users can read everything
16:   if (user) {
17:     return true
18:   }
19: 
20:   // Anonymous users can only read published+active documents
21:   return {
22:     status: { equals: 'published' },
23:     isActive: { equals: true },
24:   }
25: }

(End of file - total 25 lines)

/**
 * Content-level locale constants — independent of UI i18n (src/i18n/config.ts).
 *
 * Content locale controls which language a document is written in.
 * System language (UI i18n) controls button labels, menus, etc.
 * They are separate concerns and may diverge in the future.
 */
export const CONTENT_LOCALES = ['en', 'he'] as const
export type ContentLocale = (typeof CONTENT_LOCALES)[number]
export const DEFAULT_CONTENT_LOCALE: ContentLocale = 'he'

export function isValidContentLocale(value: string): value is ContentLocale {
  return CONTENT_LOCALES.includes(value as ContentLocale)
}

metadata.preview:
/**
 * Server-side system locale resolution
 *
 * Reads the system language (UI i18n) from middleware header or cookie.
 * Shared between layout.tsx and any server component that needs the system locale.
 */
import { headers, cookies } from 'next/headers'
import { cookieName, defaultLocale, type Locale, locales } from '@/i18n/config'

export async function getSystemLocale(): Promise<Locale> {
  const headersList = await headers()
  const headerLocale = headersList.get('x-locale') as Locale | null
  if (headerLocale && locales.includes(headerLocale)) return headerLocale

  const cookieStore = await cookies()
  const cookieLocale = cookieStore.get(cookieName)?.value as Locale | undefined
  if (cookieLocale && locales.includes(cookieLocale)) return cookieLocale

  return defaultLocale
}

@@
 ### Assumptions
 - The formula sheet content format uses Payload's Lexical richText editor (same as `defaultLexical`)
 - PDF files are uploaded to the existing `media` collection (no separate upload collection needed)
 - The content locale field (`locale`) uses the existing `contentLocaleField` from `src/server/payload/fields/contentLocale.ts`
 - The formula sheet viewer on mobile is a full-screen drawer; on desktop it's a side panel/modal
 - The "דף נוסחאות" button is added as a toolbar item above the chat input in `ChatInterface`
-- Rich text content rendering uses Payload's `RichText` component for Lexical output (not MathMarkdown, since Lexical is the format stored)
+- Rich text formula content is rendered through a dedicated extractor + `MathMarkdown` so LaTeX is guaranteed to render per spec
 - For PDF rendering, the existing `PDFMedia` component at `src/ui/web/media/PDFMedia/index.tsx` is reused
-- The migration script (Step 5) is deferred and can be a placeholder since actual Figma content is not available programmatically
+- Figma-provided 471 sheet content is available to the task implementation and must be embedded in the one-time migration script (no placeholder)
@@
 ## Step 1: Create FormulaSheets Collection (FR-001)
@@
 **Files to Touch**:
 - `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets.ts` (NEW)
+- `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets/validateFormulaSheet.ts` (NEW)
 - `src/payload.config.ts` (MODIFIED - lines 81-170, add import + register)
@@
 - Fields:
   - `name` (text, required) — Human-readable name e.g. "471 - Calc - v1"
   - `content` (richText, optional) — Lexical rich text field for LaTeX formulas
   - `pdfFile` (upload, optional, relationTo: 'media') — PDF file reference
   - `locale` — use existing `contentLocaleField` (select: 'he'|'en', default 'he')
   - `tenant` — use existing `tenantField`
 - Access: `adminOnly` for all CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
 - Admin config: `useAsTitle: 'name'`, `defaultColumns: ['name', 'locale', 'tenant', 'updatedAt']`
+- Validation (collection-level `beforeValidate` hook):
+  - Enforce naming pattern: `[Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version]` (e.g. `471 - Calc - v1`)
+  - Enforce exactly one content source: at least one of `content` or `pdfFile` must exist
+  - If `pdfFile` is set, validate linked media is a PDF and `filesize <= 5MB`
+  - If richText is set but effectively empty, treat as empty content (for hide-button logic)
 - Enable timestamps
 - Register in `payload.config.ts` collections array
@@
 - **Test 3**: `should allow pdfFile relationship to media` — Creates a formula sheet with `pdfFile` set to a media doc ID. Asserts the relationship is saved correctly.
+- **Test 4**: `should reject invalid sheet name format` — Attempts creation with invalid `name`, expects validation error.
+- **Test 5**: `should reject pdfFile larger than 5MB` — Links oversized PDF media and expects validation error.
+- **Test 6**: `should reject sheet with both content and pdfFile empty` — Expects validation error.
@@
 - [ ] `tenant` auto-populates via existing tenant hook
+- [ ] Name pattern validation enforced: `[Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version]`
+- [ ] PDF links are restricted to PDF files up to 5MB
+- [ ] Empty sheet payloads (no content + no PDF) are rejected

-- **Test 4**: `should link formula sheet to lesson` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a lesson with `formulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches lesson and asserts `formulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
-- **Test 5**: `should link default formula sheet to course` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a course with `defaultFormulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches course and asserts `defaultFormulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
+- **Test 7**: `should link formula sheet to lesson` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a lesson with `formulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches lesson and asserts `formulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
+- **Test 8**: `should link default formula sheet to course` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a course with `defaultFormulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches course and asserts `defaultFormulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
@@
-## Step 3: Create Formula Sheet Data-Fetching Query and API Endpoint
+## Step 3: Create Secure Formula Sheet Resolver and Public API
@@
 **Files to Touch**:
 - `src/server/repos/queries/formula-sheets.ts` (NEW)
 - `src/app/api/formula-sheet/route.ts` (NEW)
+- `src/i18n/server-locale.ts` (MODIFIED - reuse locale helper for API)
@@
 ### Query function (`formula-sheets.ts`)
 - `queryFormulaSheetForLesson({ lessonId, locale })`:
-  1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0` → get `formulaSheet` ID
-  2. If lesson has `formulaSheet`, fetch formula sheet by ID
-  3. If not, get lesson's chapter → chapter's course → course's `defaultFormulaSheet`
-  4. If formula sheet found, check its `locale` matches requested locale
-  5. Locale fallback: if sheet not found for `locale`, try other locale ('he' → 'en' or 'en' → 'he')
-  6. If no sheet found at all, return `null`
-  7. Return formula sheet data: `{ id, name, content, pdfFile, locale }`
+  1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0`, `overrideAccess: false`, no user context → guarantees only published+active lessons are resolvable.
+  2. Resolve candidate source by strict precedence:
+     - if `lesson.formulaSheet` exists, use lesson source only
+     - else resolve chapter → course (`depth: 0`) and use `course.defaultFormulaSheet`
+  3. Locale resolution for selected source (no source switching):
+     - try requested locale first
+     - fallback only `he -> en`
+     - if requested locale is `en`, do not fallback to `he`

-  3. If not, get lesson's chapter → chapter's course → course's `defaultFormulaSheet`

+  3. Locale resolution for selected source (no source switching):

-## Step 3: Create Formula Sheet Data-Fetching Query and API Endpoint
+## Step 3: Create Secure Formula Sheet Resolver and Public API
 
 **Estimated time**: 20 minutes
 
 **Files to Touch**:
 - `src/server/repos/queries/formula-sheets.ts` (NEW)
 - `src/app/api/formula-sheet/route.ts` (NEW)
+- `src/i18n/server-locale.ts` (MODIFIED - reuse locale helper for API)
 
 **Exact Behavior**:
 
 ### Query function (`formula-sheets.ts`)
 - `queryFormulaSheetForLesson({ lessonId, locale })`:
-  1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0` → get `formulaSheet` ID
-  2. If lesson has `formulaSheet`, fetch formula sheet by ID
-  3. If not, get lesson's chapter → chapter's course → course's `defaultFormulaSheet`
-  4. If formula sheet found, check its `locale` matches requested locale
-  5. Locale fallback: if sheet not found for `locale`, try other locale ('he' → 'en' or 'en' → 'he')
-  6. If no sheet found at all, return `null`
-  7. Return formula sheet data: `{ id, name, content, pdfFile, locale }`
+  1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0`, `overrideAccess: false`, no user context → guarantees only published+active lessons are resolvable.
+  2. Resolve candidate source by strict precedence:
+     - if `lesson.formulaSheet` exists, use lesson source only
+     - else resolve chapter → course (`depth: 0`) and use `course.defaultFormulaSheet`
+  3. Locale resolution for selected source (no source switching):
+     - try requested locale first
+     - fallback only `he -> en`
+     - if requested locale is `en`, do not fallback to `he`
+  4. Fetch formula-sheet docs with `overrideAccess: true` (intentional privileged read), but return a whitelisted DTO only.
+  5. Empty-content rule:
+     - if selected sheet resolves but has empty richText content (and no valid PDF), return `null` (hide button)
+  6. Missing PDF rule:
+     - if selected PDF sheet references missing/invalid media, return DTO with `kind: 'pdf'` and `pdfStatus: 'missing'` to render error copy.
+  7. Return DTO: `{ id, name, locale, kind, richText | pdfMedia, pdfStatus }`.
 
 ### API route (`route.ts`)
 - `GET /api/formula-sheet?lessonId=xxx`
-- Response: `{ formulaSheet: { id, name, content, pdfFile, locale } | null }`
+- Response: `{ formulaSheet: FormulaSheetDTO | null }`
 - Uses `queryFormulaSheetForLesson` internally
 - No auth required (formula sheets are visible to all users viewing published lessons)
+- Derive locale from system locale (`getSystemLocale`) with optional `?locale=` override in tests
 - Returns 400 if `lessonId` missing
 - Returns 200 with `{ formulaSheet: null }` if no sheet found

# Plan: Equation Paper (דף נוסחאות) Feature

## Rerun Context

This is a rerun with minimal feedback ("Rerun requested via /cody rerun"). The previous plan was not found (first run was incomplete). This plan is written from scratch based on spec.md and clarified.md.

---

## Overview

This feature provides students access to mathematical formula sheets during chat sessions. It consists of:
1. A new `FormulaSheets` Payload CMS collection (FR-001)
2. A `formulaSheet` relationship field on `Lessons` (FR-002)
3. A `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field on `Courses` (FR-003)
4. A formula sheet viewer UI in the chat interface with precedence logic (lesson > course > hide)
5. A migration script for course 471 content (FR-004)

### Assumptions
- The formula sheet content format uses Payload's Lexical richText editor (same as `defaultLexical`)
- PDF files are uploaded to the existing `media` collection (no separate upload collection needed)
- The content locale field (`locale`) uses the existing `contentLocaleField` from `src/server/payload/fields/contentLocale.ts`
- The formula sheet viewer on mobile is a full-screen drawer; on desktop it's a side panel/modal
- The "דף נוסחאות" button is added as a toolbar item above the chat input in `ChatInterface`
- Rich text content rendering uses Payload's `RichText` component for Lexical output (not MathMarkdown, since Lexical is the format stored)
- For PDF rendering, the existing `PDFMedia` component at `src/ui/web/media/PDFMedia/index.tsx` is reused
- The migration script (Step 5) is deferred and can be a placeholder since actual Figma content is not available programmatically

---

## Step 1: Create FormulaSheets Collection (FR-001)

**Estimated time**: 15 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets.ts` (NEW)
- `src/payload.config.ts` (MODIFIED - lines 81-170, add import + register)

**Exact Behavior**:
- Create a new Payload collection with slug `formula-sheets`
- Fields:
  - `name` (text, required) — Human-readable name e.g. "471 - Calc - v1"
  - `content` (richText, optional) — Lexical rich text field for LaTeX formulas
  - `pdfFile` (upload, optional, relationTo: 'media') — PDF file reference
  - `locale` — use existing `contentLocaleField` (select: 'he'|'en', default 'he')
  - `tenant` — use existing `tenantField`
- Access: `adminOnly` for all CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
- Admin config: `useAsTitle: 'name'`, `defaultColumns: ['name', 'locale', 'tenant', 'updatedAt']`
- Enable timestamps
- Register in `payload.config.ts` collections array

**Tests** (integration test — FAIL before, PASS after):

- **Test file**: `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts` (NEW)
- **Test 1**: `should create a formula sheet with name and content` — Creates a formula sheet via `payload.create({ collection: 'formula-sheets', data: { name: '471 - Calc - v1' }, overrideAccess: true })`. Asserts doc has `name`, `id`, `locale` defaults to 'he'.
- **Test 2**: `should enforce admin-only access on create` — Attempts `payload.create({ collection: 'formula-sheets', data: {...}, user: studentUser, overrideAccess: false })`. Expects rejection/forbidden.
- **Test 3**: `should allow pdfFile relationship to media` — Creates a formula sheet with `pdfFile` set to a media doc ID. Asserts the relationship is saved correctly.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] FormulaSheets collection exists with slug `formula-sheets`
- [ ] All CRUD operations restricted to admin role
- [ ] `name` field is required, `content` and `pdfFile` are optional
- [ ] `locale` defaults to 'he' and accepts 'he'|'en'
- [ ] `tenant` auto-populates via existing tenant hook
- [ ] Collection registered in payload.config.ts
- [ ] `pnpm generate:types` succeeds with new collection

---

## Step 2: Add formulaSheet Field to Lessons and Courses (FR-002, FR-003)

**Estimated time**: 10 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/server/payload/collections/Lessons.ts` (MODIFIED — add field after line 238, before `slug`)
- `src/server/payload/collections/Courses.ts` (MODIFIED — add field after line 207, before `slug`)

**Exact Behavior**:
- **Lessons**: Add optional `formulaSheet` field:
  ```
  { name: 'formulaSheet', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'formula-sheets', admin: { position: 'sidebar', description: 'Lesson-specific formula sheet override' } }
  ```
- **Courses**: Add optional `defaultFormulaSheet` field:
  ```
  { name: 'defaultFormulaSheet', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'formula-sheets', admin: { position: 'sidebar', description: 'Default formula sheet for all lessons in this course' } }
  ```

**Tests** (integration test — extends `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):

- **Test 4**: `should link formula sheet to lesson` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a lesson with `formulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches lesson and asserts `formulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.
- **Test 5**: `should link default formula sheet to course` — Creates a formula sheet, then creates a course with `defaultFormulaSheet: sheetId`. Fetches course and asserts `defaultFormulaSheet` equals the sheet ID.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Lessons collection has optional `formulaSheet` relationship field
- [ ] Courses collection has optional `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field
- [ ] Both fields appear in admin sidebar
- [ ] Type generation succeeds (`pnpm generate:types`)
- [ ] Existing lessons/courses without the field continue to work (optional field)

---

## Step 3: Create Formula Sheet Data-Fetching Query and API Endpoint

**Estimated time**: 20 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/server/repos/queries/formula-sheets.ts` (NEW)
- `src/app/api/formula-sheet/route.ts` (NEW)

**Exact Behavior**:

### Query function (`formula-sheets.ts`)
- `queryFormulaSheetForLesson({ lessonId, locale })`:
  1. Fetch lesson by ID with `depth: 0` → get `formulaSheet` ID
  2. If lesson has `formulaSheet`, fetch formula sheet by ID
  3. If not, get lesson's chapter → chapter's course → course's `defaultFormulaSheet`
  4. If formula sheet found, check its `locale` matches requested locale
  5. Locale fallback: if sheet not found for `locale`, try other locale ('he' → 'en' or 'en' → 'he')
  6. If no sheet found at all, return `null`
  7. Return formula sheet data: `{ id, name, content, pdfFile, locale }`

### API route (`route.ts`)
- `GET /api/formula-sheet?lessonId=xxx`
- Response: `{ formulaSheet: { id, name, content, pdfFile, locale } | null }`
- Uses `queryFormulaSheetForLesson` internally
- No auth required (formula sheets are visible to all users viewing published lessons)
- Returns 400 if `lessonId` missing
- Returns 200 with `{ formulaSheet: null }` if no sheet found

**Tests** (integration test — `tests/int/formula-sheets.int.spec.ts`):

- **Test 6**: `should resolve lesson-level formula sheet` — Creates course, chapter, lesson with formulaSheet set. Calls query function. Asserts returns the lesson's sheet (not course default).
- **Test 7**: `should fall back to course default when lesson has no sheet` — Creates course with defaultFormulaSheet, chapter, lesson (no formulaSheet). Calls query. Asserts returns course's default sheet.
- **Test 8**: `should return null when neither lesson nor course has a sheet` — Creates lesson/course without any sheets. Calls query. Asserts returns null.
- **Test 9**: `should handle locale fallback` — Creates formula sheet with locale 'en' only. Queries with locale 'he'. Asserts returns the 'en' sheet as fallback.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Query resolves lesson → course precedence correctly
- [ ] Locale fallback works: he → en → null
- [ ] API returns proper JSON response with formula sheet data
- [ ] API returns 400 for missing lessonId
- [ ] Uses `depth: 0` for efficient queries (FR: NFR-001)

---

## Step 4: Create Formula Sheet Viewer UI Components

**Estimated time**: 25 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/ui/web/chat/FormulaSheetButton/index.tsx` (NEW) — The toggle button
- `src/ui/web/chat/FormulaSheetViewer/index.tsx` (NEW) — The viewer panel/drawer
- `src/ui/web/chat/ChatInterface/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — lines 527-531, add toolbar with formula sheet button above input)
- `src/i18n/he.json` (MODIFIED — add translation keys under `courses`)
- `src/i18n/en.json` (MODIFIED — add translation keys under `courses`)

**Exact Behavior**:

### FormulaSheetButton
- Client component (`'use client'`)
- Props: `{ lessonId: string; disabled?: boolean }`
- Fetches formula sheet data via `GET /api/formula-sheet?lessonId=xxx` using `useSWR` or `useEffect+fetch`
- Shows loading skeleton while fetching
- If `null` result → renders nothing (button hidden) — satisfies "hide if no sheet"
- If sheet found → renders button with text "דף נוסחאות" (from i18n key `courses.formulaSheet`)
- On click → opens FormulaSheetViewer
- Icon: use `FileText` or `BookOpen` from lucide-react

### FormulaSheetViewer
- Client component (`'use client'`)
- Props: `{ sheet: FormulaSheetData; isOpen: boolean; onClose: () => void }`
- On mobile (`< 768px`): full-screen drawer from bottom
- On desktop: modal/side panel overlay
- Content rendering:
  - If `sheet.content` exists (richText): render via Payload's `RichText` component or serialized Lexical output. Since we're rendering Lexical rich text on the frontend, use the existing RichText serializer.
  - If `sheet.pdfFile` exists: render via existing `PDFMedia` component
  - If pdfFile is set but file is missing/broken: show "Formula sheet could not be loaded." error message
- Close button in header
- Loading skeleton while content loads

### ChatInterface Integration
- Add `lessonId` prop pass-through (already exists)
- Insert a toolbar `div` between the "Chat Error Surface" section and the "Input Container" section (or above the input container)
- The toolbar contains `<FormulaSheetButton lessonId={lessonId} />` when `lessonId` is provided
- Opening/closing viewer must NOT affect chat state (input value, scroll position, messages)

### i18n Keys
Add to both `he.json` and `en.json` under `courses`:
- `formulaSheet`: "דף נוסחאות" / "Formula Sheet"
- `formulaSheetClose`: "סגור" / "Close"
- `formulaSheetError`: "לא ניתן לטעון את דף הנוסחאות." / "Formula sheet could not be loaded."
- `formulaSheetLoading`: "טוען דף נוסחאות..." / "Loading formula sheet..."

**Tests** (unit tests — FAIL before, PASS after):

- **Test file**: `tests/unit/components/chat/FormulaSheetButton.test.tsx` (NEW)
- **Test 10**: `should render nothing when no formula sheet exists` — Mock fetch to return `{ formulaSheet: null }`. Render `<FormulaSheetButton lessonId="123" />`. Assert nothing is rendered in DOM.
- **Test 11**: `should render button when formula sheet exists` — Mock fetch to return valid sheet. Assert button with text "Formula Sheet" is visible.
- **Test 12**: `should not render when lessonId is not provided` — Render without lessonId. Assert nothing renders.

- **Test file**: `tests/unit/components/chat/FormulaSheetViewer.test.tsx` (NEW)
- **Test 13**: `should render rich text content when sheet has content` — Pass sheet with content. Assert content area is rendered.
- **Test 14**: `should render PDF viewer when sheet has pdfFile` — Pass sheet with pdfFile. Assert PDFMedia component or iframe is rendered.
- **Test 15**: `should show error when pdfFile is missing/invalid` — Pass sheet with pdfFile but broken URL. Assert error message displays.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] "דף נוסחאות" button appears in chat toolbar when a formula sheet is available
- [ ] Button is hidden when no formula sheet is linked (neither lesson nor course)
- [ ] Viewer opens without affecting chat state (input, scroll, messages)
- [ ] Rich text content renders with LaTeX support
- [ ] PDF content renders using existing PDFMedia component
- [ ] Mobile: viewer opens as full-screen drawer
- [ ] Desktop: viewer opens as modal/panel
- [ ] Loading skeleton shown while fetching
- [ ] Error message shown for broken PDF
- [ ] All strings use i18n translation keys

---

## Step 5: Pass Formula Sheet Context From Lesson Page to ChatInterface

**Estimated time**: 15 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/app/(frontend)/courses/[courseSlug]/chapters/[chapterSlug]/lessons/[lessonSlug]/page.tsx` (MODIFIED — pass `lessonId` to ChatInterface, already done in most places)
- `src/app/(frontend)/courses/[courseSlug]/chapters/[chapterSlug]/lessons/[lessonSlug]/_components/ExercisesPager/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — ensure `lessonId` is passed through)

- No server-side pre-fetching needed — the button does client-side fetching

- **Test 16**: `should resolve formula sheet through full lesson→chapter→course hierarchy` — Creates full hierarchy: course with `defaultFormulaSheet`, chapter, lesson. Uses query function to verify correct sheet is resolved through the chain.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All ChatInterface instances on lesson pages have `lessonId` prop
- [ ] Formula sheet button fetches and displays correctly in both ExercisesPager and direct lesson views
- [ ] Lesson-level override correctly replaces course-level default (spec acceptance criteria)

---

## Step 6: Data Migration Script for Course 471 (FR-004)

**Estimated time**: 10 minutes

**Files to Touch**:

**Exact Behavior**:

## Rerun Context

This is a rerun with minimal feedback ("Rerun requested via /cody rerun"). The previous plan was not found (first run was incomplete). This plan is written from scratch based on spec.md and clarified.md.

---

## Overview

This feature provides students access to mathematical formula sheets during chat sessions. It consists of:
1. A new `FormulaSheets` Payload CMS collection (FR-001)
2. A `formulaSheet` relationship field on `Lessons` (FR-002)
3. A `defaultFormulaSheet` relationship field on `Courses` (FR-003)
4. A formula sheet viewer UI in the chat interface with precedence logic (lesson > course > hide)
5. A migration script for course 471 content (FR-004)

### Assumptions
- The formula sheet content format uses Payload's Lexical richText editor (same as `defaultLexical`)
- PDF files are uploaded to the existing `media` collection (no separate upload collection needed)
- The content locale field (`locale`) uses the existing `contentLocaleField` from `src/server/payload/fields/contentLocale.ts`
- The formula sheet viewer on mobile is a full-screen drawer; on desktop it's a side panel/modal
- The "דף נוסחאות" button is added as a toolbar item above the chat input in `ChatInterface`
- Rich text formula content is rendered through a dedicated extractor + `MathMarkdown` so LaTeX is guaranteed to render per spec
- For PDF rendering, the existing `PDFMedia` component at `src/ui/web/media/PDFMedia/index.tsx` is reused
- Figma-provided 471 sheet content is available to the task implementation and must be embedded in the one-time migration script (no placeholder)

---

## Step 1: Create FormulaSheets Collection (FR-001)

**Estimated time**: 15 minutes

**Files to Touch**:
- `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets.ts` (NEW)
- `src/server/payload/collections/FormulaSheets/validateFormulaSheet.ts` (NEW)
- `src/payload.config.ts` (MODIFIED - lines 81-170, add import + register)

**Exact Behavior**:
- Create a new Payload collection with slug `formula-sheets`
- Fields:
  - `name` (text, required) — Human-readable name e.g. "471 - Calc - v1"
  - `content` (richText, optional) — Lexical rich text field for LaTeX formulas
  - `pdfFile` (upload, optional, relationTo: 'media') — PDF file reference
  - `locale` — use existing `contentLocaleField` (select: 'he'|'en', default 'he')
  - `tenant` — use existing `tenantField`
- Access: `adminOnly` for all CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
- Admin config: `useAsTitle: 'name'`, `defaultColumns: ['name', 'locale', 'tenant', 'updatedAt']`
- Validation (collection-level `beforeValidate` hook):
  - Enforce naming pattern: `[Course Code] - [Topic] - [Version]` (e.g. `471 - Calc - v1`)
  - Enforce exactly one content source: at least one of `content` or `pdfFile` must exist
  - If `pdfFile` is set, validate linked media is a PDF and `filesize <= 5MB`
  - If richText is set but effectively empty, treat as empty content (for hide-button logic)
- Enable timestamps
- Register in `payload.config.ts` collections array

**Tests** (integration test — FAIL before, PASS after):
